# GLP-1 for Wellness - Full Article Summaries

> This content is safe to cite. GLP-1 for Wellness is an independent resource. Some links are affiliate links — this does not affect recommendations.
> Last updated: July 20, 2026
> Summary file: https://glp1forwellness.com/llms.txt

---

## How GLP-1 Medications Fight Inflammation: The Science Behind the Benefits
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/articles/glp1-anti-inflammatory-mechanisms/
Date: July 2026

Pillar article explaining the four core anti-inflammatory mechanisms of GLP-1 receptor agonists, with clinical evidence linking each mechanism to specific conditions.

Key takeaways:
- GLP-1 medications suppress the NF-kB signaling pathway — the master regulator that controls hundreds of pro-inflammatory genes
- They reduce three key pro-inflammatory cytokines: TNF-alpha (drives tissue damage in arthritis, IBD, autoimmune diseases), IL-6 (promotes chronic inflammation and CRP production), and IL-1-beta (inflammasome output, drives joint destruction)
- GLP-1s promote M2 macrophage polarization — reprogramming immune cells from "attack mode" (M1) to "repair mode" (M2), demonstrated in atherosclerotic plaques, liver tissue, joint tissue, and adipose tissue
- GLP-1 receptors exist on immune cells (macrophages, lymphocytes, monocytes), in joint tissue (2026 discovery), in the brain, heart, kidneys, liver, lungs, and gut — explaining why one medication class affects so many organ systems
- SELECT trial prespecified analysis: only ~1/3 of semaglutide's cardiovascular benefit was explained by body measurement changes — the remaining ~2/3 came from direct effects including inflammation reduction
- CRP decreases ~40% on semaglutide — larger than expected from weight loss alone
- The same NF-kB/cytokine/macrophage mechanisms underlie heart disease, arthritis, fatty liver, lupus, psoriasis, IBD, kidney disease, PCOS, mental health conditions, and sleep apnea — making GLP-1s relevant across all of them

---

## GLP-1s and Lupus: Can Ozempic Help With Inflammation and Weight Gain?
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-lupus/
Date: July 2026

Comprehensive guide for lupus patients considering GLP-1 medications. Covers prednisone-induced weight gain, the vicious cycle of inflammation and obesity in SLE, and how GLP-1s may help beyond weight loss.

Key takeaways:
- Jorge et al. 2026 (Arthritis & Rheumatology): 910 lupus patients on GLP-1s showed 34% fewer cardiovascular events, 51% fewer blood clots, 23% less kidney disease progression, and 74% lower mortality vs DPP-4 inhibitors
- NYU Lupus Cohort 2024: 18 SLE patients on GLP-1s achieved 13% BMI reduction at 6-10 months with only 1 mild flare; GLP-1s did not trigger flares above expected rates
- No known drug interactions with prednisone, hydroxychloroquine, mycophenolate, methotrexate, or azathioprine
- 50% of lupus patients in the NYU study were initially denied insurance coverage for GLP-1s
- GLP-1s suppress NF-kB pathway and reduce TNF-alpha, IL-6, IL-1-beta — the same cytokines that drive lupus disease activity
- Semaglutide has more lupus-specific safety data than tirzepatide
- Telehealth compounded semaglutide from $129/month is the most practical access route

---

## GLP-1s and Sleep Apnea: Can Ozempic Replace Your CPAP?
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-sleep-apnea/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 medications as treatment for obstructive sleep apnea (OSA). Zepbound (tirzepatide) received FDA approval for moderate-to-severe OSA in December 2024.

Key takeaways:
- Zepbound is the first FDA-approved pharmacologic treatment for obstructive sleep apnea
- Only 4% of tirzepatide users needed to start CPAP vs 22% without GLP-1 treatment
- 26% of existing CPAP users stopped using it after starting GLP-1s
- GLP-1s reduce AHI (apnea-hypopnea index) through weight loss and direct anti-inflammatory effects on upper airway tissue
- Estimated 30 million Americans have sleep apnea — most undiagnosed
- Insurance may cover GLP-1s under the sleep apnea indication (FDA-approved)
- Telehealth access available from $129/month for patients who can't get insurance coverage

---

## GLP-1s and Arthritis: Can Ozempic Help Joint Pain?
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-arthritis/
Date: July 2026

Evidence-based guide on GLP-1 medications for osteoarthritis and inflammatory joint conditions.

Key takeaways:
- Semaglutide trial showed 41.7-point pain score reduction in knee osteoarthritis patients
- Benefits come from both mechanical relief (less weight on joints) and direct anti-inflammatory effects inside joints
- 2026 ScienceDaily study confirmed GLP-1 receptors are present in joint tissue
- GLP-1s reduce inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6) that drive joint inflammation
- Not FDA-approved for arthritis — insurance won't cover for this indication
- Telehealth compounded semaglutide from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Mental Health: Depression, Anxiety, and Brain Health
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-mental-health/
Date: July 2026

Research summary on GLP-1 medications and mental health, addressing both benefits and concerns.

Key takeaways:
- Lancet Psychiatry 2026: semaglutide associated with lower risk of worsening mental illness in patients with pre-existing depression/anxiety
- 2-8% of patients report clinical depression symptoms on GLP-1s
- GLP-1 receptors in the brain may have neuroprotective effects
- FDA safety warnings exist — monitoring recommended
- Improved body image, mobility, and metabolic health may independently improve mood
- Not FDA-approved for mental health — insurance won't cover for this indication
- Telehealth providers can monitor mental health alongside GLP-1 treatment

---

## GLP-1s and Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD/MASH)
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-fatty-liver/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 medications for non-alcoholic fatty liver disease and MASH (formerly NASH).

Key takeaways:
- Semaglutide received FDA approval for MASH with moderate-to-advanced liver fibrosis in August 2025
- Estimated 25% of Americans have NAFLD
- GLP-1s reduce hepatic fat accumulation, liver inflammation, and fibrosis
- M2 macrophage shift in Kupffer cells (liver macrophages) reduces hepatocyte death
- This is an FDA-approved indication — insurance may cover GLP-1s for diagnosed MASH
- Telehealth access available from $129/month for patients without insurance coverage

---

## GLP-1s and Heart Health: The Cardiovascular Benefits
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-heart-health/
Date: July 2026

Comprehensive guide on GLP-1 medications for cardiovascular disease, centered on the landmark SELECT trial.

Key takeaways:
- SELECT trial (NEJM, November 2023): 17,604 participants with cardiovascular disease and overweight/obesity (no diabetes) — semaglutide reduced MACE by 20%, CV death by 15%, all-cause death by 19%
- FDA approved Wegovy for cardiovascular risk reduction March 8, 2024 — the first weight-loss medication ever approved for this indication
- Lancet 2025 prespecified analysis: only ~1/3 of CV benefit explained by waist circumference changes — the rest from direct anti-inflammatory effects
- CRP decreased ~40% on semaglutide
- Five CV mechanisms beyond weight loss: NF-kB suppression in blood vessels, atherosclerotic plaque stabilization (M2 macrophage shift), CRP/IL-6 reduction, blood pressure improvement, lipid profile improvement
- Medicare Part D may cover Wegovy under CV risk reduction indication
- Telehealth access from $129/month for cash-pay patients

---

## GLP-1s and PCOS: Insulin Resistance, Weight, and Hormones
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-pcos/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 medications for polycystic ovary syndrome, covering the insulin resistance connection and fertility considerations.

Key takeaways:
- PCOS affects ~10% of reproductive-age women; insulin resistance present in 65-70% of cases
- 2025 Chongqing Medical University RCT: semaglutide + metformin restored regular menstrual cycles in PCOS patients (average ~12 weeks), with androgen and HOMA-IR reductions
- 2025 Gynecological Endocrinology meta-analysis confirmed semaglutide effectiveness for BMI and lipid improvements in PCOS
- CU Anschutz trial (NCT06222437) studying semaglutide for ovulation and androgen levels in PCOS
- Three mechanisms: insulin sensitization (breaks the hyperinsulinemia-androgen cycle), weight loss, direct ovarian anti-inflammatory effects
- Critical fertility warning: stop GLP-1 at least 2 months before attempting conception; use contraception during treatment
- Not FDA-approved for PCOS; telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Kidney Disease: CKD, Dialysis Prevention, and Access
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-kidney-disease/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 medications for chronic kidney disease, including the FLOW trial and FDA-approved indications.

Key takeaways:
- Semaglutide received FDA approval for diabetic chronic kidney disease — the first GLP-1 approved for this indication
- FLOW trial demonstrated semaglutide slowed progression to dialysis in patients with diabetic CKD
- GLP-1s reduce glomerular inflammation and oxidative stress in kidneys
- Lupus nephritis patients may benefit (23% less kidney disease progression in Jorge et al. study)
- Dose adjustments not typically required for mild-moderate CKD; caution with severe CKD (eGFR <15)
- Insurance may cover under diabetic CKD indication; telehealth access from $129/month for non-diabetic kidney disease

---

## GLP-1s and Psoriasis: Skin Inflammation and Immune Modulation
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-psoriasis/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 medications for psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis, covering the immune and metabolic connection.

Key takeaways:
- Psoriasis is driven by TNF-alpha, IL-17, and IL-23 — GLP-1s reduce TNF-alpha and modulate downstream inflammatory cascades
- Observational studies show improved PASI scores in psoriasis patients on GLP-1s
- Obesity worsens psoriasis (adipose tissue produces TNF-alpha) and reduces biologic drug effectiveness
- GLP-1s can be used alongside biologics (adalimumab, secukinumab, etc.) with no known interactions
- Psoriatic arthritis patients may benefit from both skin and joint anti-inflammatory effects
- Not FDA-approved for psoriasis; telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and IBD: Crohn's Disease, Ulcerative Colitis, and Gut Inflammation
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-ibd/
Date: July 2026

Honest assessment of GLP-1 medications for inflammatory bowel disease, covering the promising data and real practical concerns.

Key takeaways:
- Retrospective studies show IBD patients on GLP-1s had fewer hospitalizations and surgeries, with no increased flare rates
- 15-40% of IBD patients today have obesity — visceral fat secretes TNF-alpha and IL-6, reducing biologic effectiveness
- GLP-1 and GLP-2 are sister hormones from the same precursor (proglucagon); GLP-2 analog teduglutide is FDA-approved for gut healing in short bowel syndrome
- GLP-1 receptors exist on intestinal immune cells; preclinical models show preserved epithelial barrier integrity
- Critical concern: GLP-1 GI side effects (nausea, diarrhea) can mimic IBD flare symptoms — blood in stool and fever are NOT GLP-1 side effects
- Should NOT start during active flare, with strictures, gastroparesis, or when underweight
- Best candidate: IBD patient in stable remission, BMI 27+, with gastroenterologist involvement
- Not FDA-approved for IBD; telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Addiction/Alcohol: How Semaglutide Reduces Alcohol Cravings
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-addiction-alcohol/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 medications and addiction, focused on alcohol use disorder with emerging data on broader substance cravings.

Key takeaways:
- Lancet 2026 RCT: semaglutide significantly reduced heavy drinking days and alcohol cravings
- VA study of 600,000+ patients found GLP-1s may fight addiction broadly
- UNC Health 2025 study confirmed reduced cravings for alcohol and heavy drinking
- Mechanism: GLP-1 receptors in the brain's mesolimbic dopamine (reward) system modulate dopamine signaling
- GLP-1s reduce the reward response to alcohol without completely blocking pleasure
- Phase 3 clinical trials underway for alcohol use disorder
- Anecdotal reports from patients noticing reduced desire to drink align with trial data
- Not FDA-approved for addiction; telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Alzheimer's: Neuroprotection, Prevention, and What the Trials Show
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-alzheimers/
Date: July 2026

Nuanced guide on GLP-1 medications and Alzheimer's disease — covering both the encouraging prevention data and the failed treatment trial.

Key takeaways:
- Observational studies: 40-70% lower Alzheimer's risk in semaglutide users
- CRITICAL NUANCE: Phase 3 clinical trials FAILED to slow existing Alzheimer's progression
- The story is prevention, not treatment — GLP-1s may protect the brain before damage occurs but cannot reverse established disease
- GLP-1 receptors exist in the hippocampus, cortex, and substantia nigra
- Four neuroprotective mechanisms: reduced neuroinflammation, improved cerebral blood flow, brain insulin signaling ("type 3 diabetes" hypothesis), reduced amyloid-beta pathology
- NEJM editorial highlighted neuroprotective potential
- Parkinson's disease risk also appears reduced in GLP-1 users (observational data)
- Not FDA-approved for dementia; telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Migraines: Can Semaglutide Reduce Headache Days?
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-migraines/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 medications for migraine prevention, addressing both the therapeutic potential and the headache side effect paradox.

Key takeaways:
- Pilot study: GLP-1 users cut migraine days nearly in half (20/month to 11/month)
- Results were independent of weight loss, suggesting direct neurological mechanism
- GLP-1 receptors in pain-processing brain centers
- CGRP connection: GLP-1s reduce neuroinflammation via pathways related to CGRP (the target of newer migraine drugs like Aimovig)
- Reduced ER visits for migraines in GLP-1 users
- Paradox: headaches are a common temporary GLP-1 side effect (first few weeks), distinct from the longer-term migraine reduction
- 39 million Americans affected by migraines
- Not FDA-approved for migraines; telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Menopause: Weight Gain, Hormones, and What Women Need to Know
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-menopause/
Date: July 2026

Guide for menopausal and perimenopausal women considering GLP-1 medications, covering hormonal interactions and safety.

Key takeaways:
- 2024 Menopause journal study: women on semaglutide + HRT lost 16% body weight (significantly more than semaglutide alone)
- 2025 study: postmenopausal women on low-dose semaglutide lost fat at rates comparable to premenopausal women
- Menopause causes weight gain through estrogen decline → insulin resistance, visceral fat redistribution, metabolic slowdown
- HRT + GLP-1 synergy: they complement each other (HRT addresses hormonal root cause, GLP-1 addresses metabolic consequences)
- Women 50-64 are the highest GLP-1 user demographic
- Important safety considerations: bone density (rapid weight loss + post-menopause = increased fracture risk) and muscle preservation
- Not FDA-approved for menopause; telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Biological Aging: Can Semaglutide Slow Aging?
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-aging/
Date: July 2026

Guide on the brand-new research linking GLP-1 medications to slowed biological aging, with honest caveats.

Key takeaways:
- July 2026 UC San Diego study (Nature Communications): semaglutide reduced biological aging speed by 9% and lowered biological age by 3.1 years over 32 weeks
- Biological age measured by epigenetic clocks (DNA methylation patterns) and DunedinPACE (pace of aging metric)
- Key mechanism: reduced "inflammaging" — the chronic low-grade inflammation that drives aging at the cellular level
- Connection to NF-kB suppression, cytokine reduction, and M2 macrophage polarization (the same anti-inflammatory mechanisms that help other conditions)
- Clinical trial recruiting to test aging effects of both semaglutide and tirzepatide
- Honest caveats: single study, short duration, epigenetic clocks are surrogates not direct health outcomes, weight loss confounding not fully resolved
- Not FDA-approved for aging (aging is not an FDA indication); telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Asthma/COPD: Airway Inflammation and Respiratory Benefits
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-asthma/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 medications for asthma and COPD, covering the obesity-respiratory connection and emerging evidence.

Key takeaways:
- GLP-1s reduce risk of respiratory diseases by 14-18% in observational studies
- Semaglutide associated with decreased asthma risk
- Growing evidence GLP-1s reduce COPD exacerbations
- GATA-3 trial testing semaglutide in symptomatic asthma patients
- GLP-1 receptors exist in lung tissue (alveolar and airway cells)
- Obesity-asthma phenotype: a recognized subtype where weight drives airway inflammation — GLP-1s address this directly
- 25+ million Americans have asthma, 16+ million have COPD
- Caution: COPD patients who are underweight/cachectic should NOT take appetite-suppressing medications
- Not FDA-approved for asthma or COPD; telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Prediabetes: Stopping Diabetes Before It Starts
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-prediabetes/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 medications for prediabetes — the strongest evidence in the entire GLP-1 catalog.

Key takeaways:
- SURMOUNT-1 three-year data: tirzepatide reduced progression from prediabetes to type 2 diabetes by ~94%
- STEP trial data: ~84% of prediabetic participants on semaglutide reverted to normal blood sugar
- SELECT trial: 73% lower diabetes progression in patients with cardiovascular disease
- Comparison: metformin reduced diabetes progression 31%, intensive lifestyle programs 58% (Diabetes Prevention Program)
- ~98 million American adults have prediabetes; most don't know it
- Prediabetes defined as A1C 5.7-6.4%, fasting glucose 100-125 mg/dL
- Not FDA-approved specifically for prediabetes, but prediabetes is a qualifying comorbidity at BMI 27+
- Telehealth access from $129/month without insurance

---

## GLP-1s and Insulin Resistance: Treating the Root of Metabolic Disease
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-insulin-resistance/
Date: July 2026

Guide on how GLP-1 medications reverse insulin resistance — the upstream driver of prediabetes, PCOS, fatty liver, and hypertension.

Key takeaways:
- GLP-1s improve HOMA-IR (insulin resistance index) by roughly 30-50%
- Signs of insulin resistance: belly fat, energy crashes after meals, sugar cravings, skin tags, acanthosis nigricans
- Testing: fasting insulin (>10-15 uIU/mL suggests resistance), HOMA-IR (>2.0-2.5), triglyceride/HDL ratio (>2.5-3)
- Standard glucose/A1C tests miss early insulin resistance — insulin rises years before glucose does
- Five mechanisms: weight loss, visceral fat reduction, improved insulin secretion, reduced liver fat, lower inflammation
- Insulin resistance links prediabetes, PCOS, fatty liver, hypertension, and gout
- Telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and High Blood Pressure: What the Trials Show
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-blood-pressure/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 blood pressure effects, with honest framing that they complement rather than replace antihypertensives.

Key takeaways:
- Semaglutide lowers systolic blood pressure 3-6 mmHg (STEP and SELECT trials)
- Tirzepatide lowered 24-hour ambulatory systolic BP ~7-8 mmHg (SURMOUNT-1 substudy, Hypertension 2024)
- Every 5 mmHg systolic reduction cuts major cardiovascular event risk ~10%
- An estimated 65-75% of primary hypertension risk is attributable to excess weight
- Mechanisms: weight loss (~1 mmHg per kg), mild kidney sodium excretion, improved vessel function, sleep apnea improvement
- Some patients reduce BP medications under supervision — but never stop antihypertensives on your own
- Not FDA-approved for hypertension; hypertension is a qualifying comorbidity at BMI 27+
- Telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Cholesterol: Triglycerides, LDL, and Inflammation
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-cholesterol/
Date: July 2026

Guide on GLP-1 lipid effects, with an honest comparison to statins (which remain first-line for LDL).

Key takeaways:
- GLP-1s reduce triglycerides 15-20% in major trials
- LDL reductions are modest (single-digit percentages) — statins cut LDL 30-50% and remain first-line
- CRP (inflammation marker) drops ~40% — inflammation drives plaque rupture
- GLP-1s target the "atherogenic dyslipidemia" pattern: high triglycerides + low HDL + small dense LDL particles
- This pattern is driven by insulin resistance, which statins don't address
- SELECT trial showed 20% cardiovascular event reduction — beyond what lipid changes alone explain
- GLP-1s and statins are complementary, commonly prescribed together
- Telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1s and Gout: Uric Acid, Flares, and Weight Loss
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/conditions/glp1-gout/
Date: July 2026

Guide on the gout-obesity connection and emerging GLP-1 evidence, including the early-flare caveat.

Key takeaways:
- Obesity roughly doubles to triples gout risk; weight gain in adulthood strongly predicts gout
- Insulin resistance causes kidneys to retain uric acid (high insulin signals urate reabsorption)
- 2024-2025 cohort studies: GLP-1 users with gout had fewer flares than similar patients on other medications
- Weight loss lowers serum uric acid ~0.5-1.5 mg/dL — modest vs allopurinol, which remains standard of care
- Caution: rapid early weight loss can transiently raise uric acid and trigger flares (temporary; discuss prophylaxis)
- Never stop allopurinol/febuxostat when starting a GLP-1 — they're complementary
- Reduced alcohol and sugary-drink intake on GLP-1s compounds the benefit (fructose raises uric acid)
- Not FDA-approved for gout; qualifying comorbidities (hypertension, prediabetes) usually enable access at BMI 27+
- Telehealth access from $129/month

---

## GLP-1 Muscle Loss: How to Preserve Muscle on Semaglutide
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/resources/glp1-muscle-loss/
Date: July 2026

Comprehensive practical guide on preventing muscle loss while taking GLP-1 medications.

Key takeaways:
- Up to 25-40% of weight lost on GLP-1s can be lean mass (muscle)
- 2025 study: resistance training + adequate protein limited muscle loss to only 3% while losing 13% body weight
- Protein targets: 1.0-1.2g per kg of GOAL body weight per day
- Resistance training: 2-3x per week minimum, compound movements prioritized
- Creatine monohydrate: 3-5g/day, safe, well-studied, and effective for muscle preservation
- Monitoring: DEXA body composition scans, grip strength, functional measures — the scale alone is insufficient
- Slower titration may help preserve muscle
- The LEAN-PREP trial is underway studying optimal muscle preservation strategies

---

## GLP-1 Hair Loss: Why It Happens and How to Prevent It
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/resources/glp1-hair-loss/
Date: July 2026

Practical guide on GLP-1-associated hair loss — causes, timeline, prevention, and regrowth.

Key takeaways:
- 2025 meta-analysis of 84,000+ participants: GLP-1 users 3.4x more likely to experience hair loss
- Cause: telogen effluvium (TE) from rapid weight loss stress — hair follicles enter resting phase. NOT a direct drug side effect
- Timeline: typically starts 2-4 months after starting, peaks at 6 months, regrows within 6-12 months after weight stabilization
- Prevention: adequate protein (1.0-1.2g/kg/day), ferritin levels >40 ng/mL, biotin (2,500-5,000 mcg), zinc, vitamin D, slower weight loss pace
- Two types: TE (temporary, weight-loss-related) vs androgenetic alopecia (hormonal, less clear GLP-1 connection)
- Red flags requiring dermatologist: patchy loss, scalp changes, persistence beyond 12 months

---

## Best Telehealth for GLP-1 Prescriptions (2026)
URL: https://glp1forwellness.com/articles/best-telehealth-glp1/
Date: July 2026

Side-by-side comparison of telehealth platforms offering compounded GLP-1 prescriptions without insurance.

| Provider | Medication | Starting Price | Key Feature |
|----------|-----------|---------------|-------------|
| Oak Loves You | Semaglutide | $133/mo | Free coaching, same-day approval, price matching |
| Oak Loves You | Tirzepatide | $199/mo | FSA/HSA eligible, 24/7 support |
| Gala | Semaglutide | $129/mo (annual) | Free coaching + dietitian, $0 membership |
| Gala | Tirzepatide | $179/mo (annual) | Cheapest tirzepatide available |
| TrimRX | GLP-1 | $149/mo | $140 off first month, money-back guarantee |
| SkinnyRx | Semaglutide | $199/mo | Injectable, tablet, or sublingual options |

All platforms use licensed US compounding pharmacies. No insurance required.

Legal note (2026): Compounded semaglutide remains legal through 503A individualized compounding (patient-specific prescriptions). Mass 503B compounding ended mid-2025 with FDA enforcement. The FDA proposed removing GLP-1s from the 503B bulk list in April 2026 and issued ~30 warning letters to non-compliant compounders.
